key_insight: Layered FeMN2 nitride thin films can be synthesized by composition-tuned reactive sputtering plus NH3 annealing, but FeWN2 and FeMoN2 accommodate composition very differently in structure and properties.
engineering_relevance: Composition control offers a practical lever to tune phase purity, texture, conductivity, and magnetic response in layered nitride thin films for functional materials design.
method: Reactive sputtering followed by post-deposition NH3 annealing
Synchrotron GIWAXS to track phase formation and texture
XAS plus electrical and magnetization measurements to link local structure with properties
evidence_signal: GIWAXS/XAS and property data show broad layered-phase formation, low and composition-insensitive resistivity in FeWN2, and a resistivity maximum near stoichiometry in FeMoN2.
watch_item: Results are based on thin-film composition windows and preliminary room-temperature magnetization, so behavior may shift with processing conditions, thickness, or lower-temperature characterization.
paper_title: Composition-dependent Thin-film Synthesis of Layered Ternary Iron Nitrides FeMN2 (M = W, Mo)
